title: A Task-Oriented Few-Shot Spectroscopy Benchmark: Empirical Evaluation of Meta-Learning Approaches
description_es: Este artículo introduce un benchmark unificado para espectroscopía few-shot que consolida nueve conjuntos de datos espectrales con una pipeline de preprocesamiento consistente para evaluar métodos de meta-aprendizaje.
abstract: Spectroscopic analysis often faces the challenge of limited labeled samples. This paper introduces a unified, task-oriented benchmark for few-shot spectroscopy that consolidates nine open-access spectral datasets with a consistent preprocessing pipeline. Our empirical evaluation demonstrates that meta-learning approaches outperform traditional transfer learning across both heterogeneous and homogeneous tasks, with more structured cross-task embedding geometry.

citations.apa: Garzón, I., Gomez-Trenado, G., & Damas, S. (2026). A Task-Oriented Few-Shot Spectroscopy Benchmark: Empirical Evaluation of Meta-Learning Approaches. Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence, 167, 113593.
citations.mla: Garzón, Iván, Guillermo Gomez-Trenado, and Sergio Damas. "A Task-Oriented Few-Shot Spectroscopy Benchmark: Empirical Evaluation of Meta-Learning Approaches." Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence, vol. 167, 2026, p. 113593.
